title: HW2 Q4 — Find $i$ with $A[i] = i$ in $O(\log n)$
statement: Given a sorted array $A$ of $n$ distinct integers, find an index $i$ (if it exists) with $A[i] = i$, in $O(\log n)$.

steps.title: Key observation.
steps.body:
The array is sorted and has **distinct** integers. So consecutive elements differ by at least 1: $A[i+1] \ge A[i] + 1$.

Define $f(i) = A[i] - i$. Then $f$ is **non-decreasing**: $f(i+1) - f(i) = (A[i+1] - (i+1)) - (A[i] - i) = A[i+1] - A[i] - 1 \ge 0$.

We want the unique $i$ (if any) with $f(i) = 0$ — and since $f$ is monotone, we can binary-search for the zero crossing.

tips.label: Why distinct integers matter
tips.content: Without distinctness, $A[i+1] - A[i]$ could be $0$ and $f$ wouldn't be monotone. Distinct sorted integers force the $\ge 1$ gap.

steps.title: Algorithm.
steps.body:
Binary-search on $f(i) = A[i] - i$:

1. Base case: if range $[lo, hi]$ is empty, return $-1$ (no solution).
2. Let $i = \lfloor (lo + hi)/2 \rfloor$.
3. If $A[i] = i$: return $i$.
4. If $A[i] > i$ (i.e. $f(i) > 0$): solution, if any, is to the left; recurse on $[lo, i-1]$.
5. If $A[i] < i$ (i.e. $f(i) < 0$): solution, if any, is to the right; recurse on $[i+1, hi]$.

steps.title: Correctness.
steps.body:
By induction on range size.

**Base:** empty range → no solution → return $-1$. ✓

**Step:** consider middle index $i$.

- $A[i] = i$: done. ✓
- $A[i] > i$: for any $j > i$, $A[j] \ge A[i] + (j - i) > i + (j - i) = j$, so $A[j] > j$ → no solution to the right. Recurse left; by I.H. that call is correct.
- $A[i] < i$: for any $j < i$, $A[j] \le A[i] - (i - j) < i - (i - j) = j$, so $A[j] < j$ → no solution to the left. Recurse right; by I.H. that call is correct.

Either the algorithm finds an $i$ with $A[i] = i$, or it exhausts the range correctly returning $-1$.

steps.title: Runtime.
steps.body:
Recurrence: $T(n) = T(n/2) + O(1)$.

This is the binary-search recurrence. By recursion tree or induction: $T(n) = \Theta(\log n)$.

tips.label: Classic binary-search recurrence
tips.content: $T(n) = T(n/2) + 1 = \Theta(\log n)$. Level work is constant and there are $\log_2 n$ levels.

answerKey: Binary-search using monotonicity of $f(i) = A[i] - i$. Recurrence $T(n) = T(n/2) + O(1) = \Theta(\log n)$.
